name: dumbbell step-up
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the bench or step, straightening your right leg.
Bring your left foot up onto the bench or step, standing fully upright.
Step back down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body. Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface. Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the bench or step, straightening your right leg. Bring your left foot up onto the bench or step, standing fully upright. Step back down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, returning to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
